summary: This cluster investigates the generation of high-cell-density (HCD) phenotypes in *Chlamydomonas reinhardtii* through continuous subculture methods, which progressively increase cell-culture density to enrich for insertional mutations. Key findings link two specific HCD mutants to mitochondrial dysfunction, with inverse PCR identifying the insertion loci and suggesting gain-of-function mutations that significantly enhance biomass production compared to wild-type strains. The research is laboratory-based, focusing on the genetic and physiological mechanisms of algal growth optimization rather than a specific geographic or ecological context.
